flag_for_launching_agent.title: Scope-correction: 1.2.5 and 1.2.6 are separate issue checklist items, not one combined subtask
flag_for_launching_agent.detail: This run's original instructions described subtask 1.2.5 as a combined 'persist/reload + prefix scan' subtask (root-node-ID sidecar file, LoadRoot/SaveRoot, close/reopen round-trip test). The actual GitHub issue #2 checklist (pulled verbatim via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/issues/2 -q .body`, since `gh issue view 2`'s rendered text truncates/garbles this exact item) splits this into 1.2.5 (Prefix scan, engine/btree/scan.go+scan_test.go, -run TestPrefixScan -- this run) and a separate, still-unstarted 1.2.6 (Persisted reload correctness test, engine/btree/btree_test.go, -run TestPersistReload). This run implements only 1.2.5's actual scope. Root-node-ID persistence, LoadRoot/SaveRoot, and the close/reopen round-trip test still need to be done as subtask 1.2.6, a separate implementation run.
flag_for_launching_agent.recommendation: Schedule 1.2.6 as its own implementation run using the verbatim issue text for that checklist item as its requirement, not this run's persist/reload design guidance (which can still inform 1.2.6's design, e.g. the NodeAllocator .nodealloc sidecar idiom is a reasonable template for a root-ID sidecar, but should be re-scoped as its own one-commit subtask).

known_gaps_still_open: No persisted current-root-node-ID across process restarts (unchanged by this run; targeted at 1.2.6)
1.2.4's unreclaimed tombstoned-leaf / abandoned-merged-node-ID gap (unchanged by this run; confirmed not to affect PrefixScan correctness)
